title: HTML & CSS Fundamentals
description: Learn the building blocks of the web. This course covers semantic HTML5 and modern CSS layouts like Flexbox and Grid.

quiz.question: Which of the following is a semantic HTML5 element?
What does the CSS property 'display: flex' do?
options: Creates a flexible container for layout

quiz.question: Which HTML tag is used for the main heading in a page?
What is the difference between padding and margin?
options: Padding is outside, margin is inside
Margin is outside, padding is inside
Padding is for text, margin is for containers

quiz.question: Which CSS layout model is best for creating complex two-dimensional layouts?
What does 'responsive design' mean?
options: Design that adapts to different screen sizes

quiz.question: Which meta tag ensures proper scaling on mobile devices?
What is the CSS box model composed of?
Which HTML element defines the footer of a document?
What does CSS 'z-index' control?

title: Introduction to Front-End Development
description: Designed by Meta, this course covers how the internet works and the fundamentals of styling web pages with HTML5 and CSS3. Includes an intro to UI frameworks like Bootstrap.

quiz.question: Which protocol is used to transfer data over the web?
In the CSS Box Model, what sits between the padding and the margin?
What is the primary purpose of a 'Reset' CSS file?
options: To ensure consistent styling across different browsers

title: CS50's Web Programming
description: A deep dive into the design and implementation of web apps. Covers database design, scalability, security, and user experience using Django and React.

quiz.question: In Django, which file is primarily responsible for mapping URLs to functions?
What does a 'Migration' do in a web framework like Django?
options: Moves the site to a new server
Propagates changes you make to your models into your database schema

quiz.question: Which React hook is used to handle side effects like data fetching?

title: Full Stack Open: Deep Dive into Modern Web Dev
description: An intensive course focusing on React, Redux, Node.js, MongoDB, GraphQL, and TypeScript. Includes production-level concerns like CI/CD and Docker.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of using Redux in a large React application?
In the MERN stack, what is the role of 'Mongoose'?
options: An Object Data Modeling (ODM) library for MongoDB

quiz.question: Which of these is a key principle of RESTful APIs?

title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Explore the fundamental concepts of machine learning and build your first models.

quiz.question: What is supervised learning?
Which of the following is an example of unsupervised learning?
What is the purpose of training data in machine learning?
options: To train the model on patterns

quiz.question: What does 'overfitting' mean?
options: Model fits training data too well but performs poorly on new data

quiz.question: Which algorithm is commonly used for binary classification?
What is a feature in machine learning?
options: An input variable used for prediction

quiz.question: What does cross-validation do?
options: Tests model performance on different subsets of data

quiz.question: What is the difference between classification and regression?
options: Classification predicts categories, regression predicts continuous values

quiz.question: Which metric is commonly used to evaluate classification models?
What is the purpose of feature scaling in machine learning?
options: To normalize features to a similar range

title: Operating Systems Concepts
description: Understand the core concepts of operating systems, from processes to memory management.

quiz.question: What is a process in an operating system?
options: An instance of a program running in memory

quiz.question: What is the role of a scheduler in an OS?
options: To allocate CPU time to processes

quiz.question: What does virtual memory do?
options: Extends physical memory using disk space

quiz.question: What is a thread?
options: A lightweight process within a process

quiz.question: What is context switching?
options: Changing the context of a message
Moving data between cache and RAM

quiz.question: What is a semaphore used for in OS?
What is deadlock in operating systems?
options: Situation where processes are stuck waiting for resources

quiz.question: How does paging improve memory management?
options: Divides memory into fixed-size pages for flexible allocation

quiz.question: What is an interrupt in OS?
options: A signal that pauses CPU execution for event handling

quiz.question: What does the kernel do in an operating system?
options: Provides core OS services and manages hardware

title: Google Data Analytics Fundamentals
description: Gain an immersive understanding of the practices and processes used by a junior or associate data analyst in their day-to-day job.

quiz.question: Which of the following is the correct order of the data analysis process according to Google?
options: Ask, Prepare, Process, Analyze, Share, Act

quiz.question: In SQL, which clause is used to filter the results of a query to include only specific rows?
What is 'Data Cleaning'?
options: Deleting all data from a database
The process of fixing or removing incorrect, corrupted, or incomplete data
Moving data to a cloud server

title: Supervised Machine Learning: Regression and Classification
description: Learn the fundamentals of machine learning from the most famous course in the field. Covers gradient descent, cost functions, and regularization.

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of Gradient Descent in Machine Learning?
options: To split data into training and test sets

quiz.question: Which technique is used to prevent 'Overfitting' by penalizing large weights?
In a Binary Classification problem, which activation function is typically used in the output layer?

title: Neural Networks and Deep Learning
description: Master the theoretical foundations of Deep Learning. Build, train, and apply fully connected deep neural networks using Python and TensorFlow/PyTorch.

quiz.question: Why is the ReLU activation function often preferred over Sigmoid in hidden layers?
options: It helps mitigate the vanishing gradient problem
It always outputs a value between 0 and 1

quiz.question: What does 'Backpropagation' calculate?
options: The final accuracy of the model
The gradient of the loss function with respect to the weights
The number of hidden layers needed

quiz.question: In a CNN, what is the purpose of a 'Pooling' layer?
options: To increase the number of parameters
To reduce the spatial dimensions (width/height) of the data

title: Operating Systems and You: Becoming a Power User
description: Learn about the main components of an operating system and how to perform critical tasks like managing software and users, and configuring hardware.

quiz.question: What is the core part of an operating system that talks directly to the hardware?
In Linux, which command is used to change the permissions of a file?
What is the purpose of Virtual Memory?
options: To allow the OS to use more memory than is physically available in RAM

title: Linux System Administration Fundamentals
description: A deep dive into the Linux environment used by enterprise servers and supercomputers. Covers system architecture, disk management, and CLI proficiency.

quiz.question: Which of the following describes a 'Process' in a systems context?
options: An instance of a program being executed

quiz.question: What is the PID of the 'init' or 'systemd' process, which is the first process started during booting?
In the context of the Bash shell, what does 'piping' (|) do?
options: Sends the output of one command as input to another

title: Advanced Operating Systems & Concurrency
description: Explore the abstractions, mechanisms, and implementations of modern OS. Covers multi-threading, synchronization, and resource scheduling algorithms.

quiz.question: What is a 'Deadlock' in operating systems?
options: A computer that won't turn on
A situation where two or more processes are blocked forever, waiting for each other
A fast way to clear cache

quiz.question: Which scheduling algorithm is designed to give every process an equal share of the CPU in time slices?
What is a 'Context Switch'?
options: The process of storing and restoring the state of a CPU so that execution can be resumed from the same point

title: Foundations of Cybersecurity
description: An entry-level introduction to the world of cybersecurity. Learn how to identify common threats like phishing and malware, and understand the core principles of Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA Triad).

quiz.question: Which principle of the CIA Triad ensures that information is not altered by unauthorized users?
What is 'Social Engineering' in a security context?
options: Manipulating people into giving up confidential information

quiz.question: Which tool is commonly used by analysts to monitor and analyze security events in real-time?

title: Network Security and Access Controls
description: A globally recognized credential focusing on the technical and administrative controls used to secure networks. Covers the OSI model, encryption basics, and risk management processes.

quiz.question: What is the 'Principle of Least Privilege'?
options: Providing only the minimum level of access necessary to perform a job
Encrypting only the most important files

quiz.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for routing data packets across different networks?
What is a 'Honey Pot' in cybersecurity?
options: A decoy system used to lure and study attackers

title: Ethical Hacking & Penetration Testing
description: Move beyond theory into hands-on offensive security. Learn to think like a hacker to find vulnerabilities in web apps and networks before malicious actors do.

quiz.question: What is the first phase of a penetration test?
Which tool is standard for network scanning and service discovery?
What is a 'Reverse Shell'?
options: A shell connection initiated from the target machine back to the attacker
A way to restart a server

title: Agile & Scrum Basics
description: Learn the principles of Agile development and the Scrum framework for effective team collaboration.

quiz.question: What is the Agile Manifesto's first value?
options: Individuals and interactions over processes and tools

quiz.question: What is a sprint in Scrum?
options: A fixed time period for delivering incremental work

quiz.question: What is the role of a Product Owner in Scrum?
options: Owning the product vision and managing backlog

quiz.question: What is a daily standup in Scrum?
options: Short daily meeting to sync team progress

quiz.question: What does 'velocity' mean in Scrum?
options: Amount of work completed in a sprint

quiz.question: What is a user story in Agile?
options: A description of a feature from end-user perspective

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a sprint review?
options: To demonstrate completed work to stakeholders

quiz.question: What is backlog refinement?
options: Improving clarity and estimation of user stories

quiz.question: What does 'Definition of Done' mean?
options: Criteria that must be met for work to be considered complete

quiz.question: What is continuous improvement in Agile?
options: Regularly reflecting and improving processes and practices

title: Software Design and Architecture
description: Move beyond basic scripts. Learn how to design modular, scalable systems using UML, design patterns, and solid architectural principles like Microservices.

quiz.question: What does the 'S' in SOLID principles stand for?
Which design pattern is used to ensure a class has only one instance and provides a global point of access to it?
What is the main advantage of a Microservices architecture compared to a Monolith?
options: It is simpler to build initially
It allows services to be scaled and deployed independently

title: DevOps, CI/CD, and Software Lifecycle
description: Master the modern delivery pipeline. Learn how to automate testing and deployment using tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and Docker to ship code faster and more reliably.

quiz.question: What is the goal of Continuous Integration (CI)?
options: To write code as fast as possible
To automatically merge and test code changes frequently
To prevent users from seeing new updates
To manually review every line of code every month

quiz.question: In a CI/CD pipeline, what is 'Automated Testing'?
options: Having robots click on the screen
Running scripts to verify code functionality every time a change is pushed

quiz.question: What is 'Technical Debt'?
options: Money owed to a software vendor
The implied cost of future reworking caused by choosing an easy but limited solution now
The cost of buying new computers
A loan taken out by a tech startup

title: Algorithms & Data Structures
description: A deep dive into fundamental algorithms and data structures, the backbone of efficient software.

quiz.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
Which data structure uses LIFO (Last-In-First-Out)?
What is a hash table primarily used for?
What is the time complexity of quicksort in the average case?
What is a balanced binary search tree?
options: A tree where height difference between subtrees is minimal

quiz.question: What is dynamic programming used for?
options: Solving problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems

quiz.question: What is a greedy algorithm?
options: An algorithm that makes locally optimal choices at each step

quiz.question: Which data structure is best for implementing a priority queue?
What is Big O notation used for?
What is graph traversal used for?
options: Visiting all nodes in a graph systematically

title: Computational Thinking and Fundamentals
description: Harvard's legendary intro to CS. Learn how to think algorithmically and solve problems efficiently using low-level memory management and basic data structures.

quiz.question: What does 'Big O Notation' measure?
options: The size of a file in bytes
The upper bound of the time or space complexity of an algorithm
The number of lines of code

quiz.question: Which of the following is the time complexity of a 'Binary Search'?
In a Linked List, what is stored in a 'Node' besides the data?
options: A pointer/reference to the next node
The total size of the list

title: Algorithmic Toolbox & Data Structures
description: A deep dive into essential algorithms. Covers sorting, searching, greedy algorithms, and dynamic programming techniques used in technical interviews.

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of a Hash Table?
options: It provides O(1) average time complexity for lookups
It uses the least amount of memory
It is easier to implement than an array

quiz.question: Which algorithmic strategy involves breaking a problem into smaller sub-problems and storing the results to avoid redundant work?
In a Binary Search Tree (BST), what is true about the left child of a node?
options: It is always larger than the parent
It is always equal to the parent
It is always smaller than the parent
It is always a leaf node

title: Advanced Analysis of Algorithms
description: A rigorous mathematical approach to algorithm analysis. Covers generating functions, analytic combinatorics, and the study of intractable problems (P vs NP).

quiz.question: What does it mean if a problem is 'NP-Complete'?
options: It can be solved in polynomial time
No efficient solution is known, but a solution can be verified quickly
It is impossible for a computer to solve
It only applies to sorting algorithms

quiz.question: Which master theorem case applies to an algorithm that splits a problem into two halves and takes O(n) time to merge them (like Merge Sort)?
In the context of algorithm analysis, what is 'Amortized Analysis'?
options: Analyzing the worst-case of a single operation
Analyzing the average performance of an operation over a sequence of operations
Analyzing only the first step of an algorithm
Calculating the cost of the hardware

title: Introduction to Game Development with Unity
description: Start your journey in game dev. Learn how to navigate the Unity Editor, understand the Game Object/Component model, and write your first scripts to control player movement.

quiz.question: In Unity, what is the base class for all entities in a scene?
Which C# function is called once per frame in a Unity script?
What does a 'Rigidbody' component allow a GameObject to do?
options: Interact with the physics engine (gravity, collisions)

title: Intermediate Game Programming & Design
description: Deepen your coding skills. Learn how to handle complex game states, implement animations, and create responsive user interfaces for 2D games.

quiz.question: What is a 'Prefab' in Unity?
options: A reusable template of a GameObject and its components

quiz.question: In 2D game development, what is a 'Sprite'?
Which window in Unity is used to create and edit transitions between different character animations?

title: 3D Interaction & Virtual Reality Development
description: Master the world of 3D and VR. This course covers spatial interaction, 3D math for game devs (vectors/quaternions), and optimizing high-fidelity graphics for immersive apps.

quiz.question: In 3D math, what is a 'Quaternion' primarily used for?
options: Measuring the distance between two points

quiz.question: What is 'Draw Call Batching' in the context of game optimization?
options: Combining multiple small meshes into a single mesh to reduce CPU overhead

quiz.question: In VR, what does '6DOF' (Six Degrees of Freedom) refer to?
options: The ability to track both rotation and position in 3D space
